operation: First, a workplane is initialized on the XY coordinate plane. A regular 6-sided polygon with a circumdiameter of 20 units is sketched on this plane, then extruded vertically to a height of 10 units to form a hexagonal prism. Next, the top (maximum Z-axis) face of the prism is selected, and its six vertices are tagged as a reference. A new workplane is created aligned to these tagged top vertices, and 1.5 unit radius circles are sketched centered at each of the six vertices. A through-all cut operation is then performed to remove material along the full height of the prism at each circle location, creating vertical cutouts at each corner.
geometry: The model is a straight regular hexagonal prism with parallel matching hexagonal top and bottom faces, and six identical flat rectangular side faces. Each of the six vertical corner edges of the prism has a full-height concave semi-cylindrical cutout that runs continuously from the top face to the bottom face, centered on the original corner vertex of the prism.
function: This is a hexagonal mechanical fitting designed for use as a spacer, alignment bushing, or mounting adapter. The corner cutouts provide fastener clearance, enable positional locking, or serve as alignment references when mated with compatible hexagonal components.
